ogTitle: Eighteen Mile vs Elanora Heights: Climate Safety

heading: Eighteen Mile vs Elanora Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Eighteen Mile, New South Wales and Elanora Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Eighteen Mile has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Elanora Heights (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Eighteen Mile or Elanora Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Eighteen Mile has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Elanora Heights scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Eighteen Mile scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Elanora Heights scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Eighteen Mile or Elanora Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Eighteen Mile has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
